questions.text: A bridegroom is a/an example of:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Neuter gender is used for objects or nouns with no biological sex."Bridegroom” is not neuter because it clearly refers to a male person, so it belongs to the masculine gender.
Common gender refers to a noun that is the same whether it is referring to either gender, such as cat, people. Since,bridegroom is used specifically for man,so common gender is incorrect.

The masculine gender refers to any noun or adjective that is used to refer to people and animals classified as male.Since the bridegroom refers to only males, so masculine gender is the correct option.
The feminine gender refers to any noun or adjective that is used to refer to people and animals classified as female.Bridegroom is used specifically for males, so Option D is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The masculine gender refers to any noun or adjective that is used to refer to people and animals classified as male.Since the bridegroom refers to only males, so masculine gender is the correct option.

questions.text: Cowardice is an example of a/an _.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Common noun: While cowardice is a common noun in the sense that it's not a proper noun, the more specific classification is abstract noun.
Proper noun: Cowardice is not a proper noun because it's not the name of a specific person, place, or organization.
Countable noun: Cowardice is not a countable noun because it can't be counted or quantified (you can't have "two cowardices"). It's an uncountable or mass noun.

Cowardice is an abstract noun because it refers to a quality, state, or concept (in this case, the lack of bravery or courage) rather than a physical object or a specific person/place. Abstract nouns are intangible and can't be perceived through the senses.
